title: Characterization of mitochondrial membrane potential
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8kqhq","text":"This protocol describes the procedure used by the Seifert Lab to characterize the membrane potential of mitochondria from fibroblasts using JC-1 dye.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Seed cells on a 96-well black clear glass bottom plate at a density of ~30,000 cells per well
Culture for 24:00:00 to 48:00:00 until cells reach 80% confluency
Add 100 µL of 2 micromolar (µM) JC-1 Dye (Mitochondrial Membrane Potential Probe) Thermo Fisher Catalog #T3168 staining mix
Wash cells with warm 1X HBSS Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #H4891
Scan plates with 485 nm excitation and 529 nm detection to quantify green fluorescence (monomeric)
Wash cells in 1X HBSS Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #H4891
Optional: to measure changes in mitochondrial membrane potential in response to inhibitors
Halfway through incubation, add 1.5 micromolar (µM) Oligomycin Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #495455 and 10 millimolar (mM) 250 mg FCCP biorbyt Catalog #orb385553
Scan plates with 590 nm detection to quantify red fluorescence (aggregate)
Calculate mitochondrial membrane potential by the ratio of green to red fluorescence intensities, normalized to sample protein level (µg)
creator: Ashley Seifert
